description.en: Choose {{value}} learned skills. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for selected skills.

eval_variables.skills: expertises = selected_feats['rogue_expertise']; skills.each { |skill| next if expertises.nil?; next if expertises.exclude?(skill[:slug]); skill[:modifier] += proficiency_bonus }

description.en: Once per turn, you can deal an extra {{value}} damage to one creature you hit with an attack if you have advantage on the attack roll. The attack must use a finesse or a ranged weapon. You don't need advantage on the attack roll if another enemy of the target is within 5 feet of it, that enemy isn't incapacitated, and you don't have disadvantage on the attack roll.
You learned thieves' cant, a secret mix of dialect, jargon, and code that allows you to hide messages.
You can take a bonus action on each of your turns in combat. This action can be used only to take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.
When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to halve the attack's damage against you.

When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.
Whenever you make an ability check that lets you add your proficiency bonus, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.
If you are able to hear, you are aware of the location of any hidden or invisible creature within lO feet of you.
No attack roll has advantage against you while you aren't incapacitated.
If your attack misses a target within range, you can turn the miss into a hit. If you fail an ability check, you can treat the d20 roll as a 20.
